extraction.quote: Net income increased to $2,192, $4.93 per diluted share, compared to $1,903, $4.28 per diluted share
Net income increased to $2,192, $4.93 per diluted share, compared to $1,903, $4.28 per diluted share

validation.rule: Each metric must appear in ≥2 distinct locations OR have one high-confidence source (income statement). Material conflicts MUST be flagged.
validation.detail: All five metrics pass validation. Revenue and Net income each have three corroborating GAAP sources with exact agreement. EPS (diluted) has two agreeing GAAP sources. EPS (basic) has one high-confidence income-statement source. Gross margin has one GAAP source with a computed cross-check available. No material conflicts detected.

per_metric.reason: Exact agreement ($70,527M) across two distinct GAAP sources: Condensed consolidated statements of income (p.4) and Segment reporting (p.15). Difference = $0 (0.0%).
Exact agreement ($2,192M) across three distinct GAAP sources: Income statement (p.5), Note 7 – Net income per share (p.11), and MD&A Highlights (p.20). Difference = $0 (0.0%).

Exact agreement ($4.93) across two distinct GAAP sources: Income statement (p.5) and MD&A Highlights (p.20). Difference = $0.000. No non-GAAP alternative present; GAAP-vs-non-GAAP comparison not applicable.

Single source (Condensed consolidated statements of income, GAAP, p.5), but it is the primary income statement — a high-confidence source. No second location found in filing; confidence held at med per single-source rule.

Single source (MD&A · Gross Margin, GAAP, p.22). Implied cross-check: $7,635 gross profit / $70,527 revenue = 10.83%, a ~0.21pp difference from the stated 11.04% — within rounding tolerance given possible intra-period adjustments. No second explicit gross-margin location found; confidence held at med.

narrative: No prior-quarter or prior-year figures were provided, so relative comparisons cannot be made. Revenue was $70.5B, net income was $2.19B, diluted EPS was $4.93, and gross margin was 11.04% for the current quarter.

confidence.summary: The dominant confidence driver is the clean GAAP-only EPS (eps_gap: null), which, combined with strong intra-document corroboration (9 corroborations, 0 conflicts, 3 high-conf metrics), supports a solid baseline. Confidence is modestly trimmed by single-period cross-source coverage (only 1 period available) and a thin, near-zero price-trend dataset that cannot contribute meaningfully to alignment.

Validation passed with zero flagged conflicts. 9 corroborations were found across 5 distinct sources covering 5 metrics. The confidence tally shows 3 high, 2 medium, and 0 low items — an entirely clean profile. No integrity concerns in this factor.

The single EPS reading is $4.93 with 0 sign reversals and 0 periods with low-confidence metrics — all internal signals are clean, but the single-period limitation prevents a meaningful consistency assessment. factors.name: Insider activity & news
factors.signal: Mixed — one insider sale of ~$5.77M; headlines broadly constructive for Costco's business narrative
factors.detail: Over the 90-day insider window: 0 buys, 1 sell with a net value of -$5,774,295. A single insider sale of this magnitude at a large-cap company (consistent with Costco's scale) is not inherently alarming but does provide no corroborating buy-side conviction. Recent headlines are predominantly Costco-specific and largely constructive (membership model strength, parking/checkout improvements, interest-rate resilience), with no headlines contradicting the reported financial picture. A few tangential headlines (Kroger, Target, SNAP, EV savings) are irrelevant noise. Overall, insider and news signals are mildly mixed but not concerning.
